Details: Removal of metolachlor with PAC is effective (70 to 100 percent removal) under a range of water quality parameters and PAC sorption conditions in bench-scale studies with waters taken from four surface waters. Higher PAC dose and more contact time increased removal; depressing the pH or increasing the NOM concentration decreased removal. In full-scale applications, metolachlor removal ranged 43 to 94 percent and was dependent on the PAC dose.
